response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is an experienced web and full-stack engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement — which he does not possess (his languages are English, Romanian, and basic German). Beyond that disqualifier, the role demands deep C programming for embedded UNIX/Linux firmware, Linux kernel internals, and hands-on security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, etc.); none of these are present in any meaningful way in his history. His brief C/C++ work at National Instruments (7 months) and a student thesis are insufficient for a mid-senior embedded security position.
strengths: Long and varied career (15+ years) demonstrating consistent ability to learn new stacks and domains
Mentioned embedded systems in his professional summary and has some C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows, thesis project with Linux + JACK audio)
Comfortable operating in Linux environments and has shipped production software across multiple platforms
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement for the role
No security domain knowledge: zero mention of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, authentication systems, or any network security protocol
No embedded networking or switch/firmware experience; career is overwhelmingly web, cloud, and application-layer work

key_factors.factor: No security or embedded networking expertise
Insufficient C and Linux kernel depth
